author: zhuochun
text: [Ruby] Build with custom location cannot load successfully

author: zhuochun
text:
I am following <https://docs.travis-ci.com/user/migrating-from-legacy/> to build grpc to `$HOME/grpc_build` and then `bundle install` with `gem 'grpc', '~> 0.11'`

My grpc build script and I have set global variable `GRPC_ROOT="$HOME/grpc"`

```
#!/bin/sh
set -e

# check to see if grpc folder is empty
if [ ! -d "$HOME/grpc_build/lib" ]; then
    git clone --depth 1 -b release-0_11_0 https://github.com/grpc/grpc.git "$HOME/grpc"
    cd "$HOME/grpc" && git submodule update --init
    make && make install prefix="$HOME/grpc_build"
else
    echo 'Using cached grpc directory.';
fi
```

bundle install grpc is succeed. But load grpc result in error:

author: zhuochun
text: My goal is to use travis container-based infrastructure, which doesn't allow `sudo`. Seems `make install` is not achievable without `sudo`.

author: ctiller
text:
We'll be removing the need to install grpc core for Ruby shortly (most of  
it is already there at head), which should alleviate this problem for you.

author: murgatroid99
text: murgatroid99 added lang/ruby lang/all wrapped languages on Jan 4, 2016

author: murgatroid99
text:
First, it looks like that grpc build actually succeeded. The message at the end is just a warning, and does not actually affect the Ruby library.

Second, you are installing the grpc core, so setting `GRPC_ROOT` is not only unnecessary, but actively harmful. You should instead set `PKG_CONFIG_PATH="$PKG_CONFIG_PATH:$HOME/grpc_build/lib/pkgconfig"`.

When you set `GRPC_ROOT` to a grpc clone *and* `pkg-config` does not find the existing grpc installation (which it would not do by default because of the custom install location), the Ruby library links against `$GRPC_ROOT/libs/opt/libgrpc`. Because you already ran `make`, it links with `libgrpc.so`. Then, when you try to run it, it cannot find `libgrpc.so` in the default shared library search path.

As ctiller mentioned, we will eliminate this problem in the next release (0.12)

author: zhuochun
text: Thanks [@robert-w-gries](https://github.com/robert-w-gries) and [@murgatroid99](https://github.com/murgatroid99) for the help. I will try release 0.12 and see how things go.
